title: orchis papilionacea subsp. chlorotica
intro: Orchis papilionacea subsp. chlorotica, also known as the green-veined orchid, is a sub-species of the bee orchid (Orchis papilionacea), a type of terrestrial orchid native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of the green-veined orchid include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as having antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the roots, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions.
